races.race_name: Weddings At Western House Hotel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mind Over Matter
runners.spotlight: Raced three times last term, clear best effort when second of seven over C&D (good) on debut; major player if back to that form, and this reappearance may be the time to catch him; hood applied.
runners.comment: Best 2yo effort when second over C&D on debut; major player if back to that form

runners.name: Stoneacre Joe
runners.spotlight: Gelded after sole 2yo start; belied his 100-1 odds with second of 14, behind clearcut winner, at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) on reappearance; one of the main contenders, provided that form is backed up.
runners.comment: One of the main players, provided reappearance effort at Doncaster is backed up

runners.name: Railwayman
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling purchase whose siblings include dual Group 1 sprint scorer Oxted; showed promise with 2l third of 13, behind two previous winners, in Leicester contest (6f, good; wore tongue-tie); respected with progress on the cards.
runners.comment: Nicely bred; showed promise with third of 13, behind previous winners, at Leicester
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Le Puy
runners.spotlight: Finished fourth of six at Catterick (7f) on reappearance, managing to improve on her 2yo C&D effort but recording only a modest RPR; needs further improvement to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Needs further improvement to get off the mark

runners.name: Thistle Nil
runners.spotlight: 4yo gelding by U S Navy Flag out of 6f-winning (RPR 74) half-sister to useful winners Taexali (5f 2yo) and Caliburn (7.7f-1m4f); sole newcomer in the field and it's a belated debut; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Sole newcomer in the field and it's a belated debut; likely best watched

runners.name: Wee Dresser
runners.spotlight: Plenty to find on her 7f AW efforts at Newcastle; this new scenario needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Plenty to find on her 7f AW efforts at Newcastle

runners.name: April Diamond
runners.spotlight: 5yo mare who was always towards the rear at Newcastle (6f, AW) on belated debut, beaten 6l and recording a modest RPR; likely one for handicaps further down the line.
runners.comment: Recorded a modest RPR at Newcastle on belated debut

runners.name: Mac's Wee Sister
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly who trailed home last of seven at Southwell (7f, AW) early this year for an inauspicious belated debut and sole start for Iain Jardine; needs a marked step forward for her new yard.
runners.comment: Last of seven at Southwell in sole start for Iain Jardine

runners.name: Saxon Gem
runners.spotlight: Always behind in C&D maidens last May/June, beating a total of one rival; likely to find better opportunities in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Always behind in C&D maidens last term

races.race_name: Half Price Brunch/Lunch @westernhousehotel Handicap

runners.name: Canaria Queen
runners.spotlight: Improved on reappearance run to win at Doncaster last month (5f, good to firm; second won next time); up 2lb; strong chance.
runners.comment: Beat a subsequent winner at Doncaster last time and has a chance off this 2lb higher mark

runners.name: Ski Angel
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who won twice at Musselburgh last season; ideally suited by good ground or softer but lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; won twice at Musselburgh in 2026; best on good or softer; may need it

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Newcastle in January (5f); mixed form since, one of her best efforts when third over 6f at Thirsk last time (good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Off the mark on AW in January; mixed form since (fair third latest); each-way chance

runners.name: Little Mi Mi
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; had a good winter on the AW for Iain Jardine and has returned from a two-month break with two more sound AW runs; 0-10 on turf, although has run well on occasions; chance.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; has a good winter for new, including after a break; 0-10 on turf but a chance
runners.owner: N Irving, F Steele & I Jardine

runners.name: Harb
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off this mark last August (good); also won at Newcastle in February; needs to bounce back after two below-par runs.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2025; also won on AW in February; below-par last twice; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Keep The Gold
runners.spotlight: Unexposed sort; fair second in a Redcar maiden handicap last June (5f, good to firm; cheekpieces on for first time) but has been absent since.
runners.comment: Unexposed; fair 2nd in maiden h'cap at Redcar last June but has been absent since

runners.name: Bonito Cavalo
runners.spotlight: Won six times at Newcastle from 5f-7f early in 2024; 0-27 on turf and well beaten last five starts.
runners.comment: Six AW wins in early 2024; 0-27 on turf and has been out of form on AW; others stronger

runners.name: Fear And Fast
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; ran a couple of fair races for Jim Goldie last term after being bought for 10,000gns in July; perhaps best watched after a 181-day break.
runners.comment: 0-16; ran a couple of fair races after 10,000gns buy in July but lacks a recent run
runners.owner: Let's Be Lucky Racing 38 & Steven Wilson

runners.name: Royal Duke
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last July (good) and also won at Musselburgh last August; form tailed off towards the end of the season and now back on his last winning mark; lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: Two wins, one over C&D last year; back on his last winning mark but lacks a recent run

runners.name: Tommy McJohn
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; won a Naas nursery (5f, good) off a 13lb higher mark; some fair efforts since, including when third at Dundalk in January; quite interesting on stable debut.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; won Naas nursery in 2024; fair run on AW latest; a possible on stable debut

races.race_name: Sunday Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap

runners.name: Little Ted
runners.spotlight: Into the veteran stage but on a competitive mark on form as recent as December; recent Redcar run should have sharpened him up but others may have his measure for win purposes.
runners.comment: On a fair mark but needs to step up on recent Redcar run and he is 0-15 at 7f

runners.name: Ashen
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but he has not been with Jim Goldie long and his latest Musselburgh third (1m, good) was encouraging; Paul Mulrennan replaces an apprentice today and a bigger effort could be forthcoming.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden but latest Musselburgh third (1m, good) was promising; more to come

runners.name: Jkr Cobbler
runners.spotlight: Four times a C&D winner, the latest by half a length on soft ground last September; 4lb lower today and of obvious interest from a handicapping perspective; could probably do with some rain arriving though.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins; on a good mark but good to firm ground wouldn't be optimal

runners.name: Coconut Bay
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a purple patch last summer, winning three 7f handicaps (including here); 5lb lower than for the last of them and last month's return to action should have sharpened her up.
runners.comment: C&D winner; 5lb lower than for last success; sharper for last month's return; contender

runners.name: Camera Shy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for new connections when leading late on in a 7f handicap at Southwell in March; not quite so good twice since but he still commands plenty of respect in this field.
runners.comment: 7f AW win in March; not quite so good twice since but still commands respect
runners.owner: The Horse Watchers 13 And Michael Dods

runners.name: Habrdi
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who was a ready winner at Wolverhampton in March; similar form wheen fourth at Southwell on his next start but his performance has dipped twice since; no guarantee this will be run to suit.
runners.comment: Not at best on his last two starts and no guarantee he'll get a good pace to aim at here
runners.owner: Mrs S D Pearson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Approaching Dawn
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much in his four starts, including his handicap debut at Musselburgh last week (7f, good), but he's bred to do better at some point; stable also run Ashen.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much, including on last week's handicap debut, but should do better in time
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Trais Fluors
runners.spotlight: Getting on for three years since his last turf success and this veteran hasn't been at his best at Newcastle of late; opposable back at 7f and returned to the grass.
runners.comment: His last five wins have come on AW at Newcastle; opposable back at 7f on turf

races.race_name: Luxury Breaks At Western House Hotel Handicap

runners.name: Land Of The Giants
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden but came close at Cork last time when neck second in an 18-runner handicap (1m, good to yielding); contender.
runners.comment: 0-17; beaten a neck in an 18-runner h'cap at Cork last time and has a chance on that form
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Nigel O'Hare & Philip Smith & Bart O'Sullivan

runners.name: Glasses Up
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has won 11 times here, including three last year (1m2f/1m5f); on a good mark and should come on for his run at Musselburgh last week; contender.
runners.comment: 11-times course winner (three last year); should improve on his run last week; contender

runners.name: Millbuie
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden; had a wind op in November and ran one decent race on the AW in the winter (1.25l fourth of nine over 9.5f at Wolverhampton in February); below par next time; lacks consistency.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden; had wind op in November and one good run since; inconsistent
runners.owner: N J Dalgarno & W R Ellis

runners.name: Uncle Liam
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; runner-up on his last three starts last season, all with give in the ground, including when beaten a nose at Hamilton in September (8.5f, good to soft); lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: 0-9; second on last three starts in 2025, all with give in the ground; lacks a recent run

runners.name: Dingwall
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m2f handicap here in July 2024 (1m, good) but last two wins have been at Wolverhampton; three sound runs on the AW on last three starts and one to consider.
runners.comment: Won over 1m2f here in 2024; has been in good form on AW this year and one to consider

runners.name: Royal Blaze
runners.spotlight: Winner over 1m2f at Ayr (good; cheekpieces on) and Newcastle (tongue-tie and cheekpieces) last term; some fair AW runs after that but off since January and perhaps best watched this time (no cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Two wins in cheekpieces last term, including here; back after a break with no headgear

runners.name: Zebra Star
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f here on his seasonal reappearance in 2024 (good) and was fourth over 1m here after a break in May last year, so could go well despite lack of a recent run.
runners.comment: Won on reappearance here in 2024 and decent run after a break in 2025; could go well

runners.name: Golden Valour
runners.spotlight: Not a regular winner; successful at Musselburgh last September (1m, good) but form tailed off after that and lacks a run this time.
runners.comment: Musselburgh winner last September; form tailed off after that; lacks a recent run

runners.name: Tap Dancer
runners.spotlight: Bought for 8,000gns out of Marcus Tregoning's yard last October having won a five-runner Bath novice over 1m2f in testing ground in 2024; didn't progress in handicaps last year but quite interesting off a decent mark on stable debut.
runners.comment: Bought 8,000gns in October having won 1m2f Bath maiden; quite interesting in stable debut

runners.name: Shifter
runners.spotlight: Out of form in three runs on the AW during the winter (latest over 1m4f) but is better on turf (won twice over 1m2f in May 2024); could go well at a decent price.
runners.comment: Out of form on AW in the winter; better on turf (two wins in May 2024); could go well

runners.name: Runninsonofagun
runners.spotlight: Only win was in a maiden claimer at Navan (1m, good) in August 2024 when trained by Tony Mullins; three good runs on the AW when last seen out (October) and one to consider.
runners.comment: Won a Navan claimer for Tony Mullins in 2024; in good form on AW when last out; a possible

runners.name: Lovette
runners.spotlight: Won a Ripon maiden last July (1m, good) and best subsequent run was when third in a Carlisle handicap in September (1m, soft); could go well on second run at this trip, but has a 190-day absence to overcome.
runners.comment: Ripon maiden winner last July; also 3rd in Carlisle handicap; 190-day absence to overcome

races.race_name: Book Unite Scotland Summer Raceday Handicap

runners.name: Eve's Boy
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate is only 1-16 but he gained the win at Ayr and returns here off an attractive mark (peak one was 15lb higher); ran encouragingly at Thirsk (1m, good; 1l second) most recently, proving suited by the switch back to turf from AW.
runners.comment: Gained sole win at Ayr and returns here off a handy mark; ran encouragingly last time

runners.name: Novak
runners.spotlight: Has done his winning over 6f/7f, the last five times on AW (hasn't scored on turf since 2023); kept mostly to Newcastle since last September; not the percentage call in this scenario.
runners.comment: All wins over 6f/7f, the last five on AW; not the percentage call

runners.name: Rajapour
runners.spotlight: Only 1-19 in Britain but he proved resurgent and looked unlucky in new headgear at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) nine days ago, going very close despite meeting traffic issues; respected off same mark in retained cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Proved resurgent and looked unlucky in first-time cheekpieces at Wetherby nine days ago

runners.name: Starliner
runners.spotlight: Ran encouragingly at Hamilton on Sunday when switched back to turf (from AW) for first time since September; still has a modest strike-rate but is well treated on his win off 10lb higher last May.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly on Sunday returned to turf; won off 10lb higher last May
runners.owner: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt

runners.name: Penelope's Sister
runners.spotlight: Gained all three of her 2025 wins over this C&D, the most recent on penultimate start; no certainty to make a successful reappearance, having never scored when fresh, but market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Gained all three of her 2025 wins over C&D; has never scored when fresh; reappearance

runners.name: Donna Nook
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-18 and she looks more reliable on AW than on turf; however, has frame possibilities provided her recent consistency is transferred back to this sphere; close third at Southwell (1m) last week.
runners.comment: Has frame possibilities provided he recent consistency on AW is transferred back to turf

runners.name: Pearl Eye
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful for Darryll Holland, then form went markedly awry during a brief spell with Gay Kelleway; sold for 13,000gns since last run; market may prove instructive on debut for another new yard.
runners.comment: Fairly useful on his day; market may prove best guide on debut for another new yard

races.race_name: Ayr Racecourse Racing Club Handicap

runners.name: The Gay Blade
runners.spotlight: Successful three times over 6f on Newcastle AW this year; ran well at Musselburgh (7f, good; neck second) returned to turf last week; now 2lb higher but Cara Tuke removes 7lb; respected in current form.
runners.comment: Three AW wins this year; ran well at Musselburgh returned to turf last week

runners.name: Tarlac
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-17 and there is a question mark over his current form; however, a revival would put him firmly in the picture; gained his success off 8lb higher last May.
runners.comment: Revival would put him in the picture; won off 8lb higher last May
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Colour Code
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on form and registered sole success in AW classified event; refused to race in March and was withdrawn having bolted before the start at Hamilton on Sunday.
runners.comment: Refused to race in March and withdrawn having bolted before the start at Hamilton Sunday

runners.name: Filly's Last Lady
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who is gradually coming back to form for new stable, respectable fifth at Catterick (6f) most recently; may add to last year's two summer wins if the resurgence continues.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; gradually returning to form for new stable; possibilities

runners.name: Taygar
runners.spotlight: Record is 4-28; has form figures of 113 at Ayr, the wins gained off current mark and 6lb higher; interesting returned to this course with Thirsk reappearance (faded late in big field) under her belt.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 113 at Ayr; interesting off a handy mark back here

runners.name: Summerstorms Dream
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden in her first season last year, latterly running encouragingly at Ayr (6f/5f) in her two handicap attempts, both in July; may take a further step forward this term; one to consider.
runners.comment: Has run encouragingly at Ayr in both handicap attempts; may do better still

runners.name: Iris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Has form at several different venues but is ideally suited by Hamilton, having done nearly all of her winning there; below par last time, failing to back up reappearance effort; 0-9 at Ayr.
runners.comment: Has done nearly all of her winning at Hamilton; 0-9 at Ayr

races.race_name: Book Sounds Of Summer Racenight Handicap

runners.name: Tupero
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; off the mark at Newcastle in February (1m4f) and a creditable second to a subsequent winner back on turf at Catterick next time (1m4f, good); bit disappointing at Southwell last week, but interesting now up 3f in trip.
runners.comment: Ex-French; 1m4f AW winner in February; steps up in trip after below-par run last week

runners.name: Ebony Maw
runners.spotlight: Has won three times here, the latest over this trip in October 2024 (good); placed on several occasions without winning last term but was behind Tupero on his reappearance at Catterick last month (1m4f, good).
runners.comment: Three wins here, latest over 1m7f; bit to find with Tupero on last month's Catterick form

runners.name: Elemental Eye
runners.spotlight: Winner at Redcar (2m, good to soft) and Musselburgh (1m6f, soft; by 10l) last term; went hurdling in the winter; fair run back on at Flat at Newcastle in March (1m4f); chance, particularly if the ground eases.
runners.comment: Two wins last season, the second by 10l (soft ground); could go well if the ground eases

runners.name: Aighear
runners.spotlight: Has won three times here, all with give in the ground, twice over this trip; would be interesting if the ground eases but usually comes on for her first run.
runners.comment: Goes well here (three track wins); best with some give in the ground; may need the run

runners.name: Grey Fable
runners.spotlight: Irish raider; won twice over 1m4f at Chester in July 2024 (good/good to soft); mixed efforts since, mainly at 1m2f/1m4f as he didn't seem to stay when tried at 2m.
runners.comment: Irish raider; two Chester wins over 1m4f in 2024; mixed form since; stamina to prove

runners.name: Cascade Hall
runners.spotlight: 20-race maiden on the Flat; ran a couple of fair races over 1m4f/2m at Musselburgh last summer, but his form tailed off after that; lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: 0-20; two fair runs at Musselburgh last summer but form tailed off; lacks a recent run

races.race_name: Weatherbys nhstallions.co.uk Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Prince Rhinegold
runners.spotlight: Only win came in a novice back in 2023; nearly added a handicap in March (good to soft) but he has his share of off days.
runners.comment: Nearly won a handicap in March (good to soft) but he has his share of off days
runners.owner: Mrs D Robinson Mrs & Mrs Livesey-Van Dorst

runners.name: Berkshire Smudge
runners.spotlight: Sole win came here on the Flat over 1m2f; has shaped well enough in three hurdle runs to believe he could have a future off this sort of mark.
runners.comment: Flat winner; sufficient promise over hurdles to be of interest on this handicap debut
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Penn Avenue
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner; no easy task in a maiden last time and has run well in a couple of handicaps; could have a part to play if reacting positively to the new tongue-tie.
runners.comment: No easy task in a maiden latest and has run well in two handicaps; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Forever Penywern
runners.spotlight: Needed sellers to win on the Flat for William Haggas; four runs over hurdles, the standout being his 7l third in a juvenile race at Stratford; struggled since, latterly on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Selling winner on the Flat; early hurdle form lacks substance; second run in a handicap

runners.name: Collanisi
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 15 but beaten 21l in his latest maiden at Wetherby (2m, good); handicaps are now a better option and his bumper form reads quite well.
runners.comment: Bumper form reads well; fourth in latest maiden; handicap debutant

runners.name: Model Approach
runners.spotlight: Well treated if going back far enough but achieved little for current yard, over hurdles and fences; finished last of three on final run.
runners.comment: Well treated if going back far enough but achieved little for current yard

runners.name: Kates Choice
runners.spotlight: First placing when second at Exeter (2m, good) but was some 25l off the winner; dropped 2lb.
runners.comment: First placing when second at Exeter (2m, good) but was some 25l off the winner

runners.name: Get The Value
runners.spotlight: Exposed 8yo who has topweight and he's looked out of sorts in four runs following a break.
runners.comment: Exposed 8yo who has topweight and he's looked out of sorts in four runs following a break

runners.name: Uncle Remus
runners.spotlight: Won a good-ground bumper last summer; needed to up his game on handicap debut in October but was beaten 13l at 16-1.
runners.comment: Needed to up his game on handicap debut in October but was beaten 13l at 16-1

runners.name: Walk In The West
runners.spotlight: Kicked off his handicap career with a promising second at Taunton (2m, good) in March and never really figured when held up over 2m4f a week later; back in trip after a month off, he could easily be very competitive.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; back in trip after a month off, he could easily be very competitive

runners.name: Bowling Shane
runners.spotlight: Limited in four Flat runs and tailed off in three runs over hurdles; plenty to prove on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Limited in four Flat runs and tailed off in three runs over hurdles; handicap debut

runners.name: And She Was
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced but allowed to go off at 50-1 on handicap debut in January (2m, good to soft) and was pulled up; hard to be confident.
runners.comment: Allowed to go off at 50-1 on handicap debut in January (2m, good/soft) and was pulled up

runners.name: Peace Motivator
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper for Olly Murphy; pulled up in his latest maiden on soft ground but was running well on good at his point of departures previously; has since left Alison Thorpe and makes stable/handicap debut after a lengthy break; market could be revealing.
runners.comment: Has ability but makes stable/handicap debut after a lengthy break

races.race_name: Mickey Bowen 51 Winners Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Fairye Forth
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who divided good horses in a C&D novice two weeks ago (good to soft) and that marks him down as the standout candidate.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who divided good horses in a C&D novice two weeks ago

runners.name: Getaway Vic
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who went 2-3 in good-ground bumpers; absent for over a year but should pay his way over hurdles.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who went 2-3 in good-ground bumpers; absent for over a year

runners.name: Idaho Fire
runners.spotlight: Second in a point and his bumper run wasn't devoid of promise; however, has stopped quickly in two maiden hurdles, with and without a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Has stopped quickly in two maiden hurdles, with and without a tongue-tie

runners.name: Mariniere
runners.spotlight: Beaten 20l and more in his three bumpers and limited appeal on hurdle debut with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l and more in his three bumpers and limited appeal on hurdle debut

runners.name: Kind Intention
runners.spotlight: Definite promise in all three bumpers that were won by smart horses, including Storming George and Mydaddypaddy; this longer trip for hurdle debut is an obvious move on pedigree; has been off since last April.
runners.comment: Definite promise in all three bumpers that were won by smart horses

runners.name: Yellow Card
runners.spotlight: Quite useful on the Flat but was 300-1 for a recent novice here and a long way behind Fairye Forth.
runners.comment: 300-1 for a recent novice here and a long way behind Fairye Forth

races.race_name: James Bowen Ton Up Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Les's Destiny Star
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper scorer; had wind surgery prior to a resurgent effort, finishing 5l third and one place behind Star Turn, over C&D (good to soft) last month in only his second handicap attempt; warrants respect off same mark, with a step forward possible.
runners.comment: Had wind surgery prior to resurgent effort over C&D last time; respected off same mark

runners.name: Constantinian
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who is a bumper winner; respectable third at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft; handicap debut) in latest hurdles attempt; may show progress, provided he stays this new trip.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; ran respectably on handicap debut last time; steps up in distance
runners.owner: The St George & The Dragon Partnership

runners.name: Hillwalk
runners.spotlight: Kept to shorter under rules but was in the process of chasing down a smart rival when falling in Irish point in sole 3m attempt; creditable second to thriving opponent at Chepstow (2m3f; handicap debut) in latest hurdles attempt; interesting back up in distance.
runners.comment: Looks interesting in this second handicap attempt and first crack at 3m under rules

runners.name: Star Turn
runners.spotlight: 6yo maiden who ran well, one place in front of Les's Destiny Star, in first-time visor over C&D (good to soft) last month on debut for new stable and return from lengthy layoff; likely player provided that effort is backed up and the bounce is avoided.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D last month on stable debut and return from absence; likely player

runners.name: Jaja Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on form and folded tamely when upped to 3m1f (from 2m5f) in first-time cheekpieces at Huntingdon most recently; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form and folded tamely upped to 3m1f most recently

runners.name: Colby Mayfield
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has shown improvement under 5lb claimer since switched to handicaps, fifth at Chepstow (2m7f) last time; may take another step forward, with Sam Twiston-Davies booked; not ruled out.
runners.comment: May take another step forward, with Sam Twiston-Davies replacing 5lb claimer

runners.name: Times Are Green
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has failed to complete in two starts since handicapping; otherwise interesting on bits of bumper/hurdles form and this is a drop in class.
runners.comment: Has failed to complete in two starts since handicapping but this is a drop in class

runners.name: Dessie Haze
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 8yo who opened his account in Chepstow event (2m7f, good to soft) then finished midfield, not disgraced, over C&D (good) later in April; reasonably solid in current form.
runners.comment: Opened his account in Chepstow event; midfield over C&D later in April

runners.name: Parliamo
runners.spotlight: Has stamina in his pedigree but didn't strictly improve for a step up to 2m7f at Bangor (handicap debut) most recently; however, still ran respectably and may build on that effort.
runners.comment: Ran respectably upped to 2m7f at Bangor (handicap debut) most recently

races.race_name: Sean Bowen Champion Jockey Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hell Hound
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate- Hell Hound

races.race_name: Gwyn Brace Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Makinbecon
runners.spotlight: Has offered little in his four runs, only ninth of 14 in a 3m handicap three weeks ago; tries cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Has offered little in his four runs, only ninth of 14 in a 3m handicap three weeks ago

runners.name: This Sway
runners.spotlight: Rain welcomed as the ground was soft when making all at Southwell in March (2m) but beaten 11l and 23l in his subsequent races in new cheekpieces; dropped 3lb for those, so only 4lb above his win; wouldn't be a certain stayer over this far.
runners.comment: Winner in March; below that level since and wouldn't be a certain stayer over this far

runners.name: Bells Of Ufford
runners.spotlight: Perhaps best on goodish ground so the weather needs watching; it was good to soft when a 5l fourth in this new headgear combination at Chepstow in March and some late interference didn't help; chance.
runners.comment: Late interference no help when a 5l fourth in this new headgear combination last time

runners.name: Jukebox Joker
runners.spotlight: Won at Warwick (2m3f, good to soft) and then ran well behind next-time-out winner at Sedgefield (2m4f, good to soft); a chance on all that and his Ludlow run in February was too bad to be true.
runners.comment: Last run was too bad to be true, but had been in excellent form prior to that

runners.name: Kylenoe Dancer
runners.spotlight: Has won a handicap (off 6lb lower) but there are long absences in her profile and she was pulled up when returning from one at Fontwell in March.
runners.comment: Absences in her profile and was pulled up at Fontwell when back from the latest

runners.name: Mind Hunter
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for ages but latest 9l defeat over C&D was after a lengthy break; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Hasn't won for ages but latest 9l defeat over C&D was after a lengthy break

runners.name: Lion Of The Desert
runners.spotlight: Failed to stay 3m two runs back and then made all over C&D two weeks ago, scoring by 2l (several hurdles were omitted); off only 3lb higher he should be on the scene once more.
runners.comment: Failed to stay 3m two runs back and then made all over C&D two weeks ago; up 3lb

runners.name: Alba Crus
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner on good ground; two underwhelming runs over hurdles but the middle effort was respectable and he now enters handicaps; this mark is no gift, however.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; middle hurdle run was respectable and now enters handicaps

runners.name: Dublin To Milan
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden over hurdles who has shown no worthwhile form in three handicap chases since February; tries blinkers, but cheekpieces did nothing for him.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden over hurdles who has shown no worthwhile form in three handicap chases

runners.name: Albert Park
runners.spotlight: No better for cheekpieces or this headgear combination in his last three races and he's now 0-15; unconvincing.
runners.comment: No better for cheekpieces or this headgear combination in his last three races

runners.name: Mancero
runners.spotlight: French hurdles winner; building some momentum and was only 2l off the front-running Lion Of The Desert here a fortnight ago; 2lb better off with the winner.
runners.comment: Building some momentum and was only 2l off the front-running Lion Of The Desert here

runners.name: Play On Court
runners.spotlight: Showed more in the new blinkers when beaten 13l in a 2m Exeter maiden but produced a lesser performance next time and now goes handicapping with much to prove.
runners.comment: Only the one effort of any real note and goes handicapping with much to prove

races.race_name: Adept GRP Cabinets Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Lelant
runners.spotlight: Two good-ground wins during the winter but nothing positive to take from his last two races and the weather forecast doesn't bode well.
runners.comment: Dual winner but nothing positive to take from his last two races

runners.name: Boston Joe
runners.spotlight: Goes well here, gaining fourth course success with a narrow verdict in March (2m3f, soft); beaten 8l behind L'Empire Vert off this 4lb higher mark on his return visit but he was held up in a race that favoured those ridden handier; good chance.
runners.comment: Goes well here and latest fifth at the track can be upgraded; chance

runners.name: Loki's Mischief
runners.spotlight: Placed over hurdles and again on his chase debut at Exeter when fitted in a first-time hood; that aid is missing here but stepping up from 2m3f looks favourable.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles and again on his chase debut at Exeter; steps up in distance

runners.name: Of Corse I Can
runners.spotlight: Won two 3m2f handicap chases in October but went a bit cold afterwards and he's back from a lengthy break here.
runners.comment: Won two 3m2f handicap chases in October but went a bit cold afterwards

runners.name: Bahtiyar
runners.spotlight: 9lb out of the weights when not beaten far at Warwick 12 days ago (2m4f, good); 1lb lower here but rain would scupper his chance.
runners.comment: Encouraging second at Warwick 12 days ago but rain would scupper his chance

runners.name: Jack's A Legend
runners.spotlight: Point/hurdle winner; only his third run over fences when beaten 3.25l at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good to soft) and he clouted the second last; considered.
runners.comment: Clouted the second last when beaten 3.25l at Newton Abbot (2m5f)

runners.name: Radharc Na Slaine
runners.spotlight: 1-14 as a chaser; won at Stratford in September (2m6f, good) and fair efforts since then; can go well if fitness isn't an issue on first run since October.
runners.comment: Running well when last seen and likely to feature if fitness isn't an issue

runners.name: Jack's Jury
runners.spotlight: Stable debut when winning a 2m4f hurdle here in March (soft) but hasn't built on that, albeit after going up 7lb; remains to be seen what he'll make of fences.
runners.comment: Course winner over hurdles but remains to be seen what he'll make of fences

runners.name: L'Empire Vert
runners.spotlight: Second career win when making pretty much all here last month (2m3f, good to soft); effectively 9lb higher as a 3lb claimer was employed last time, while stamina for this far is unproven; still needs a second look given current wellbeing.
runners.comment: Made all here in April; effectively 9lb higher as a 3lb claimer was employed last time
runners.owner: E A R Morgans, C A Pengelly, G Wilson

runners.name: Sawpit Samantha
runners.spotlight: 0-14 over hurdles and has finished ahead of one rival in his two chase starts; more new headgear tried from 6lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-14 over hurdles and finished ahead of one rival in his two chase starts; new headgear

runners.name: Rangatira Jack
runners.spotlight: 1-19 overall, though the win did come over fences; however, hasn't fired for a while, despite attracting market support on his return to hurdles last time.
runners.comment: 1-19 overall, though the win did come over fences; however, hasn't fired for a while
runners.owner: Mrs D Robinson Mrs & Mrs Livesey-Van Dorst

races.race_name: Societies Welcome At Gowran Park Maiden

runners.name: The Publican's Son
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but rated 100 and has some smart stakes form in the book; disappointing that he couldn't justify favouritism in a 1m Cork median auction race last time but looks well worth giving another chance to dropped back to 7f.
runners.comment: 0-4 but smart stakes form in the book, beaten fav' over 1m latest, drop to 7f a plus

runners.name: Starred
runners.spotlight: All three starts over 6f at the Curragh; Ran poorly when 50-1 on debut last June; stronger in the betting the next twice and ran much better, beaten just 1l when third of 16 behind Mumhan (yld-sft) and beaten 2.25l when runner up behind Silk Braid (yielding); should appreciate the step up to 7f and big chance if ready after break.
runners.comment: Solid 6f maiden efforts at the Curragh last year, 7f should suit, big chance if fit

runners.name: Bamako Beach
runners.spotlight: Excellent neck second of 16 on debut at Naas (7f, yld-sft) last September, headed close to home; off since and is hooded for return; leading claims if ready to rock after 229-day absence.
runners.comment: Fine second on debut at Naas last September, now hooded, leading claims if fit

runners.name: Starflow
runners.spotlight: No impact on debut at Dundalk in December and same story on seasonal return here last month; tongue tie needs to have a dramatic effect.
runners.comment: Poor form in both starts, tongue tie needs to transform

runners.name: Glasarmi
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army filly; dam unplaced 6f-1m (RPR 42), half-sister to winners Dark Regard (6f inc 2yo), Pride Of Nepal (1m1f-1m4f) and Michonne (French 1m3f/2m); likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Invincible Army filly, dam of little account, likely best watched on debut
runners.owner: P R Dowling & Ms G Brennan

runners.name: Sancerre Rock
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye making late gains after a tardy start on debut at Limerick 10 days ago when a 5.25l sixth of 10 at 22-1; should be more streetwise now and he could go well.
runners.comment: Eyecatcher after slow start at Limerick on debut, more streetwise now, could go well

runners.name: Shaws Bay
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 17-2 on debut over C&D (soft) last month and shaped nicely enough before fading late into an 8.5l fourth of 13; should improve for that initial experience.
runners.comment: 17-2 for debut over C&D (soft) and ran respectably, should improve

runners.name: Absoluteassignment
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit more when a 7.5l seventh of 10 at Limerick last time but will probably be seen to better effect when tackling handicaps.
runners.comment: Improved effort at Limerick last time but probably one for handicaps

runners.name: Rodeo Blues
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and failed to beat a rival home on debut at Leopardstown last month; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 28-1 and last on debut at Leopardstown, others make more appeal
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited

runners.name: Spiritofthedawn
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on debut at Leopardstown last June and similar outcome on seasonal return at the Curragh last month; unlikely.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both starts at Leopardstown and the Curragh, unlikely

runners.name: Casla's Eagle
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and midfield on debut over 1m here on soft ground; same story at Cork on Sunday; much more needed.
runners.comment: 100-1 and midfield on debut over 1m here, same outcome at Cork on Sunday, much more needed

runners.name: Cosmic Funk
runners.spotlight: 20-1 and never landed a blow here on debut 13 days ago; will need to improve plenty on that to get involved.
runners.comment: 20-1 and never involved here on debut 13 days ago, much more needed

runners.name: Ragaire
runners.spotlight: Sent off at just 15-2 on debut at the Curragh 16 days ago but was always behind and beat just one of his 24 rivals home; can only be watched after that tame effort.
runners.comment: 15-2 but only beat one of 24 rivals home on debut at the Curragh, can only watch now

races.race_name: Thomastown Maiden

runners.name: Kalir
runners.spotlight: Rated 78 after three runs; just mild promise both starts last year but improved effort on seasonal return at Dundalk when a 1.5l third; rolled to the right that day so cheekpieces look a prudent addition; big player.
runners.comment: Rated 78, improved effort at Dundalk latest, cheekpieces go on, big player
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Loch Gamhna
runners.spotlight: Standout effort on second start last season in a Curragh maiden (7f, sft-hvy) when a 2.25l third behind Causeway and Iron Lily when 100-1; soundly beaten on seasonal return at the Curragh (7f, sft-hvy) and well held in auction race at Cork last time; a bit to prove for now.
runners.comment: Standout effort in hot Curragh maiden last year, not as good this season, a bit to prove

runners.name: Western Bandit
runners.spotlight: Poor run over 9.5f here on debut early last month; much more like it when running on for a 3.5l fifth of 15 in a Limerick maiden 10 days ago (1m, yielding); chance if repeating that in first-time tongue tie, though is giving weight away to younger rivals.
runners.comment: Left debut run behind when fifth at Limerick latest, chance with repeat, tongue tied

runners.name: Stellar View
runners.spotlight: Has run to a similar level on both starts; 5.5l fifth of 14 on debut at Dundalk in November (7f); similar result on second start over this C&D (soft) last month; sharper for that and could run into the money.
runners.comment: Beaten similar distances both starts, sharper for C&D run latest, could run into the money

runners.name: Casamorati
runners.spotlight: Has run to a modest level in maidens here (9.5f, soft) and when dropped to 7f at Limerick last time; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Modest form in both maidens, qualifies for handicaps after this

runners.name: Saliba
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns gelding; dam placed 1m-1m2f (RPR 75), half-sister to US Graded stakes-placed 8.5f/9.5f winner Toofareastiswest, out of US 8.5f winner; market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: Ten Sovereigns gelding, dam placed am-1m2f, market best guide on debut

runners.name: Satoyama
runners.spotlight: Blew the start so did well in the circumstances to finish a 9l fifth of 13 on debut here last month (7f, soft) when a 7-2 shot; step up to 1m should suit on breeding and could go well if he gets away on level terms.
runners.comment: Ran ok after blowing the start here on debut, could go well if gets away on level terms

runners.name: Will You Stop
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior gelding; bought back for E50,000 as a yearling; half-brother to winners Gulya (French 7f; RPR 93) and Marroof (KSA 6f/1m); dam unraced sister to Irish 2,000 Guineas winner Romanised; appeal on paper and worth a market check for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Saxon Warrior gelding, dam sister to Romanised, worth market check on debut

runners.name: Hippology
runners.spotlight: Kept on for a 5.25l fifth of 15 on debut at Navan (1m, yielding) last October; sent off the 13-8 favourite on sole start since back at Navan (1m2f, heavy) in March (lost a shoe); surely capable of better than that but risky for now.
runners.comment: Promise on debut, soundly beaten fav' on latest, capable of better but risky for now

runners.name: Matada
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and beaten 22l on debut at Navan in March; may need more time.
runners.comment: 50-1 and no show on debut at Navan, may need more time

runners.name: Captain Con
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on debut at the Curragh last July (7f, good); tailed off on first run since at Navan (1m2f, heavy) in March; hard to fancy on the back of that effort.
runners.comment: Tailed off on return at Navan, hard to fancy on the back of that effort

runners.name: Dun Aonghasa
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and beaten 8.25l on debut in a 6f Dundalk maiden last month; likely best watched now stepping up in trip and switched to turf.
runners.comment: Modest fifth over 6f on debut at Dundalk, watch upped in trip on turf debut

runners.name: Leeward
runners.spotlight: Moderate efforts in maidens at the Curragh (1m2f, sft-hvy) and Cork (10.5f, gd-yld); drop in trip should suit but hard to make a case for on form.
runners.comment: Moderate efforts in maidens at the Curragh and Cork, hard to fancy on form

runners.name: Faithful Follower
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler hasn't looked like adding to his tally in three starts on the Flat; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler, hasn't looked like adding to tally on Flat, look elsewhere

runners.name: Rolltight
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten when 16-1 on sole start for Jessica Harrington at Bellewstown last year; best watched on seasonal reappearance and stable debut.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten sole start last year, best watched on seasonal return and stable debut

runners.name: Chico Chico
runners.spotlight: Impossible to fancy on the evidence of three runs last season.
runners.comment: Impossible to fancy on what he showed in three starts last year

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Vintage Tipple Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Snapretend
runners.spotlight: Won twice last season, a Punchestown maiden (9.5f, gd-yld) and a conditions race at Bellewstown (12.5f, soft); reeled in late on by Moody in Cork Listed race on return (1m4f, sft-hvy); 3lb better off for that 0.5l defeat so could turn that form around but not guaranteed to stay 1m6f on breeding; the choice of McMonagle.
runners.comment: 3lb better off for 0.5l defeat by Moody latest, stamina to prove, preferred by McMonagle

runners.name: Floresta
runners.spotlight: Progressive last season; two wins from five starts including a Listed contest at Leopardstown (1m6f, good) by 4.25l; signed off with a very respectable 3.75l fifth in the Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster (14.5f, gd-sft); carries a penalty but big chance if ready after 236 days off.
runners.comment: Progressive, Listed win last year, fine effort in Gr'2 Park Hill, 3lb penalty, big chance

runners.name: Moody
runners.spotlight: French recruit got off to a perfect start for Paddy Twomey, landing a Listed heat at Cork (1m4f, sft-hvy), beating Snapretend by 0.5l; 3lb worse off with that rival now and is moving up in trip but her dam won over 1m6f so there's every chance she'll stay; headgear worn last time is retained.
runners.comment: Won 1m4f Cork Listed on stable debut, should stay this far, carries 3lb penalty, chance

runners.name: Nuit
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been highly tried at times to no avail but did pick up some black type when a 5.25l third behind Floresta in a Listed heat at Leopardstown (1m6f, good) last season; beaten in a Dundalk maiden when last seen in December; ground a concern on return; tongue tie goes on.
runners.comment: Maiden, Listed placed behind Floresta last season, ground a query here, tongue tied

runners.name: Jannah Star
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and rated 76; placed in all four maidens last year at 1m4f on varying ground; fourth in Bellewstown maiden latest; steps up in trip and class now; hard to make a case for on form.
runners.comment: 0-6 and rated 76, beaten in Bellewstown maiden latest, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Bay Colony
runners.spotlight: 1-8 on the turf, the win coming in a 1m4f Limerick maiden last season (1m4f, sft-hvy); decent run in premier handicap at the Curragh last time (1m2f, sft-hvy) but much more needed in this company; well beaten four previous starts in stakes races and stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Maiden winner, decent handicap run on latest, much more needed, stamina to prove

runners.name: Dippedinmoonlight
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; point-to-point and bumper winner; 1-3 on the Flat; beaten off 86 in a 1m6f handicap at Navan (heavy) latest; major improvement needed in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, beaten off 86 in handicap on latest, lots more needed in new cheekpieces

runners.name: Contorsion
runners.spotlight: French recruit one of four for yard; won maiden by 10l on debut for Andre Fabre at Saint-Cloud (1m4f, soft); suffered odds on defeat stepped into Listed company at Longchamp (1m4f, gd-sft); bought for E100,000 at Arqana in December; new trip here and overlooked by McMonagle so likely best watched after 320-day absence.
runners.comment: French maiden winner, bought for E100,000, new trip, overlooked by McMonagle, watch

runners.name: Denim And Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Sole success in Limerick maiden last June (1m4f, good); didn't really progress from that and finished well beaten both tries in Listed company last year; below best on seasonal return in a handicap here (1m4f, soft); this looks too demanding.
runners.comment: Limerick maiden winner, struggled both previous starts in Listed company, up against it

runners.name: Tamam Desert
runners.spotlight: No win since landing a Galway maiden in 2024; well beaten on first two starts for this yard in Curragh handicaps at 1m and 1m2f on testing ground; hood worn for those runs discarded and upped in trip; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No win since 2024, well beaten in Curragh handicaps last twice, hard to fancy

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Victor McCalmont Memorial Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Faiyum
runners.spotlight: Won three of four starts last season, including a C&D Group 3 by 7l on heavy ground; also placed in a Naas Group 3 on good ground; solid return when a 2.5l third in the 1m Park Express at the Curragh last month; step back up to this trip a plus and should be too good for these, even giving weight away.
runners.comment: C&D Group 3 winner last year, pleasing run on return last month, the one to beat

runners.name: Lemsairbat
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate of 1-18 but has run plenty of solid races in defeat, including in Listed company; fine effort on seasonal return when 3l behind Beset in Listed heat at Naas (10.5f, soft); not beaten far when fifth of 12 in Longchamp Listed race latest (10.5f, gd-sft); place chance but overlooked by McMonagle.
runners.comment: 1-18 but lots of solid efforts in defeat including in Listed company, place chance
runners.owner: H R D McCalmont & Hamish McCalmont & Andrew N C Bengough

runners.name: Abbey Actress
runners.spotlight: Solid form in handicaps for John O'Donoghue; solid efforts in handicaps at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy) and Leopardstown (1m2f, yld-sft) on first two runs for this stable; up against it on the figures here but if there's heavy rain she could sneak into the money.
runners.comment: Solid form in handicaps, up against it on the figures here, best on very testing ground

runners.name: She's A Nation
runners.spotlight: Impressive 5l Curragh maiden winner over 1m (good) on debut last June; 10l third in heavy-ground C&D Group 3 next time out; trailed in last of 10 on seasonal return in Group 3 at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy) but was reportedly showing signs of being in season; tongue tie now goes on; could improve for return to a sounder surface.
runners.comment: 10l third in C&D Gr' 3 last year, poor seasonal return, return to better ground could help

runners.name: Apercu
runners.spotlight: Four turf wins have come in handicaps, the latest in May last year at the Curragh off 92 (1m2f, good); beaten 3l off 92 on return at the Curragh 11 days ago; has come up short when tried in stakes company, including when down the field in this race last season; needs more.
runners.comment: Four-time handicap winner, soundly beaten in this last year, needs more

runners.name: Lady Lilac
runners.spotlight: Killarney maiden winner touched off on seasonal return in handicap at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy) off 80; beaten 3l off 4lb higher at Limerick in first-time cheekpieces (discarded) 10 days ago; this looks too ambitious.
runners.comment: Maiden winner, rated 84, this looks too ambitious, cheekpieces worn latest discarded

runners.name: Sindria
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown maiden winner for Dermot Weld; no joy first two starts for this stable but off the mark on seasonal return in a 1m handicap here (soft) off 82; moves up in trip and steps into pattern company for the first time; interesting that McMonagle prefers her to Lemsairbat; place claims.
runners.comment: Impressive 1m winner here latest, up in trip and grade, preferred by McMonagle

runners.name: Um Wadi
runners.spotlight: AW winner is 0-8 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; rated just 77 and beaten in a Limerick handicap on latest; surely out of her depth in this company.
runners.comment: 0-8 on turf, beaten in Limerick handicap latest, rated 77, out of her depth here
runners.owner: Mark Feighery & Kevin Blake & Paul McKeon

runners.name: Subsonic
runners.spotlight: Got to within 0.5l of the top class Minnie Hauk on debut at Leopardstown in 2024 (1m, soft); off the mark on her fourth and final start of 2025 in a 1m2f Curragh maiden (yld-sft); rated 88 and this looks too big an ask on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Won Curragh maiden on final start last year, rated 88, this looks too big an ask

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Almeiyda
runners.spotlight: Beaten over 10 lengths on both outings at 1m; Irish Oaks entry looks lofty but trip should help.
runners.comment: Irish Oaks entry has been beaten over 10 lengths on both outings over 1m; trip should help
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Dark Lucinda
runners.spotlight: Second foal; E40,000 Y; closely related to 5f 2yo winner Wondrous Ways (RPR 54); dam 1m2f winner (89), half-sister to French 1m2f Listed winner Wonderful Times, out of 7f 2yo Group 3 winning sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Gustav Klimt; hood on for debut.
runners.comment: Second foal; E40,000 Y; closely related to 5f 2yo winner; dam 1m2f winner
runners.owner: Robert J W Moran & Ballylinch Stud

runners.name: Jolie Cailin
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; E44,000 Y; half-sister to winners Tomahawk King (7f AW 2yo; RPR 76), Berry Edge (1m1f/1m2f Flat inc AW, 74; 2m hurdle) & Al Zaker (Hungarian 7f); dam 1m winner (78), out of 7f/8.4f Listed winning half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 2 winner Volochine.
runners.comment: Fifth foal; E44,000 Y; half-sister to three winners out of 1m winner

runners.name: Mathoura
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; E30,000 F, E65,000 Y; half-sister to French winners Illois (9.5f/1m2f AW) and Imbleville (10.5f AW); dam 9.5f-10.7f winner (inc AW), out of Group-placed 1m1f-10.5f winning half-sister to 1m2f BC Filly & Mare Turf winner Starine.
runners.comment: Fourth foal; E65,000 Y; half-sister to two French winners out of 9.5f-10.7f winner

runners.name: Shanala
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to winners Sharlouk (Listed-placed 6f/7f inc HK; RPR 107), Sharinay (1m/1m1f; 104) and Shaiyhar (French/Australian 1m2f-14.5f); dam 7f winner (92), closely related to 1m winner Sherkali, out of half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners inc Sharlouk (Listed-placed 6f/7f) & Sharinay (1m/1m1f; 104)
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Spring Sprinter
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to yard's 1m2f-12.4f Flat (RPR 99) and useful 2m-2m2f hurdle winner Smooth Tom, half-sister to 1m6f Flat (89) and useful 1m7f-2m1f hurdle/chase winner Royal Hollow; dam unplaced 7f-11.3f (57), half-sister to useful jumper Viaduct Joey.
runners.comment: Related to yard's useful dual-purpose performers Smooth Tom and Royal Hollow

runners.name: Vauntingly
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; 110,000gns Y; half-sister to winners Hype (6.7f-1m2f inc 2yo/AW/Australia; RPR 95) and Diamond Ranger (7f/11.4f inc AW; 95); dam placed 1m4f (80), half-sister to 5f/6f Group 1 winner Kingsgate Native.
runners.comment: Fifth foal; 110,000gns Y; half-sister to a pair of middle-distance winners

runners.name: Venerini
runners.spotlight: Out the back when sent off 300-1 for Leopardstown debut.
runners.comment: Out the back when sent off 300-1 for Leopardstown debut

runners.name: Pure Valour
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well held on first two outings.
runners.comment: Big prices and well held on first two outings.
runners.owner: Mrs Annette Coogan & J M O'Reilly & Frank Condra

runners.name: Grennanstown Lady
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three hurdle outings and same story on Flat debut at Bellewstown.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three hurdle outings and same story on Flat debut at Bellewstown.

races.race_name: Follow Us On Instagram Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Neon Eyes
runners.spotlight: Three 7f runs at Dundalk over the winter, best of them when placed on debut; hood goes on for first handicap.
runners.comment: Three 7f runs at Dundalk over the winter, best of them when placed on debut; first h'cap

runners.name: Cause I Like You
runners.spotlight: Just one moderate run on turf; other than that, modest form at Dundalk, led when beaten little over 4l (1m) last time.
runners.comment: Modest form at Dundalk, led when beaten little over 4l (1m) last time.

runners.name: Beau Army
runners.spotlight: Has won over 6f/7f at Dundalk and runner-up over 1m there last time; probably didn't like soft ground when beaten 9l here on second-last outing.
runners.comment: Solid record on AW; soft ground unhelpful when beaten 9l here on second-last outing

runners.name: Aurora Mist
runners.spotlight: Yet to place but ran okay in back-end nurseries; 4-1 fav for course handicap over 7f last month, weakened out of contention; first try at 1m.
runners.comment: Ran okay in back-end nurseries; 4-1 fav for course handicap over 7f last month, weakened

runners.name: Ramair
runners.spotlight: Good run at 50-1 when third over this trip on handicap debut at Dundalk; hasn't built on that, latterly when tried over 10.5f.
runners.comment: Good run at 50-1 when third over this trip on h'cap bow at Dundalk; hasn't built on that

runners.name: Tenacious
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run at Thurles on final maiden start at two; well beaten in pair of AW handicaps this term.
runners.comment: Not a bad run at Thurles on final start at two; well beaten in pair of AW handicaps

runners.name: Femme Beauty
runners.spotlight: Fairly exposed after 11 starts; mark had been in decline until C&D third in claimer, under this inexperienced rider, saw it bumped back up by 7lb.
runners.comment: Fairly exposed after 11 starts; mark went back up 7lb after third in C&D claimer

runners.name: Iryna's Star
runners.spotlight: Already looks pretty exposed after 11 starts, best effort when third to Beau Army over 7f on AW in January.
runners.comment: Already looks pretty exposed after 11 starts, best effort when third over 7f on AW

runners.name: He's Holding Court
runners.spotlight: Second Slattery-trained runner; keen and made a lot of the running when beaten 4.5l against older rivals over 1m at Cork, only giving best 1f out; not a bad run at all in the context of this race, and marksman dropped her 4lb; chance if settling better.
runners.comment: Ridden prominently when beaten 4.5l against older rivals at Cork; dropped a handy 4lb

runners.name: Honey Dale
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run at all when fifth over 7f here on soft, as the winner was thrown in; first start at 1m, and hasn't been shaping as if she wants the extra distance.
runners.comment: Not a bad run at all when fifth over 7f here on soft, as  winner was thrown in

runners.name: Ochtar Againn
runners.spotlight: Three maiden outings over 6f and middle run at the Curragh wasn't terrible; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Three maiden outings over 6f and middle run at the Curragh wasn't terrible; handicap debut

runners.name: No Bother Aoife
runners.spotlight: Second Cromwell-trained runner; unfancied for three AW maidens around the turn of the year; didn't show loads but there should be better to come now that she has a mark; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Unfancied for three AW maidens and didn't show loads;  better to come now given a mark

runners.name: Millhone
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to yard's 1m handicap winners Morse (RPR 85); may improve for entering handicap company.
runners.comment: Half-sister to yard's 1m handicap winner Morse (RPR 85); may improve for handicap company
runners.owner: N J Hitchins & Mrs John M Weld

runners.name: Believe Believe
runners.spotlight: Ran okay in 7f nursery here and well backed 4-1 fav at Galway next start, would have been a few places closer but for meeting trouble in running; cheekpieces tried there are left off for this return; hasn't fully shown his hand yet, and has been found a winnable contest if he's ready; draw has been kind too.
runners.comment: Ran okay in 7f nursery here; 4-1 fav at Galway next start, but met trouble in running

races.race_name: Save 33% Buying Tickets Online Handicap

runners.name: Skib Gold
runners.spotlight: Won at a monster price over 9.5f here in July and second back here next time; shaped like he needed comeback last month; likes good ground.
runners.comment: Won at a monster price over 9.5f here last summer;  shaped like he needed his comeback

runners.name: Can I Kiss You
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in eight starts; one of her better efforts when beaten 4l at Thurles in October; off since.
runners.comment: Unplaced in eight starts; one of her better efforts when beaten 4l at Thurles in October

runners.name: Kitty Bear
runners.spotlight: Has won twice for Orla Tynan, including 3l defeat of Majestic King over 7f here last month when ridden more patiently; up 8lb for that.
runners.comment: Has won twice for Orla Tynan, including 3l defeat of Majestic King over 7f here last time
runners.owner: Charles Kerwin & Brendan Kerwin & Liam Kehoe

runners.name: Carvalhal
runners.spotlight: Winner over 1m1f on quick ground in Britain; placed three times in a row (including over C&D) last June when headgear went on; form tailed off in the autumn.
runners.comment: Placed three times in a row (including over C&D) last June when headgear went on

runners.name: Shahada Ace
runners.spotlight: Bolted up in 7.5f handicap at Tipperary last summer but has been disappointing since; prefers good ground; handicapper starting to relent.
runners.comment: Bolted up in 7.5f handicap at Tipperary last summer but has been disappointing since

runners.name: Ballymagreehan
runners.spotlight: Best form on AW at Dundalk, a winner and twice placed over this trip; off three months and there's a race for him at Dundalk later this month.
runners.comment: Best form on AW at Dundalk, a winner and twice placed over this trip; off three months

runners.name: Young Churchill
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner last summer; best Flat form is over farther, but poor last twice and doubtful that dropping back to 1m is the answer.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner last summer; best Flat form is over farther, but poor last twice

runners.name: Famous Enough
runners.spotlight: Can start slowly but often comes home well; two wins over this trip and would have been up in another stride or two at Dundalk last time; goes well at this, his local track, too.
runners.comment: Can start slowly but often comes home well, two wins over 1m and near-miss on AW latest

runners.name: Hale Bopp
runners.spotlight: No win since 2023 and best on AW, but ran okay over 1m at Cork last time when beaten a little over 3l in 18-runner contest.
runners.comment: No win since 2023 and best on AW, but ran okay over 1m at Cork last time

runners.name: Bang Po
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 2023 and no worthwhile form last year or this.
runners.comment: Sole win in 2023 and no worthwhile form last year or this.

runners.name: Half Nutz
runners.spotlight: Ages since his last win and comes out of the stalls in his own time before plugging on without threatening; something similar likely here.
runners.comment: Ages since his last win; comes out of the stalls in his own time before plugging on

runners.name: Majestic King
runners.spotlight: Well-bred Godolphin cast-off kept on takingly, after a slow start, to be second to Kitty Bear over 7f on handicap debut here; far from hard-ridden on that occasion and 1m should suit; must be a big player.
runners.comment: Kept on takingly, after a slow start, to be second to Kitty Bear over 7f on h'cap debut

runners.name: Staywitherjohnny
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens but has disappointed in three handicaps on soft ground; better for last month's return, at least.
runners.comment: Promise in maidens but has disappointed in three handicaps on soft ground

runners.name: Thegooseiscooked
runners.spotlight: Belatedly came good last year with a string of placed efforts and a win over 9.5f here (beating Skib Gold); not as effective over longer trips after and has been off since falling over hurdles in October.
runners.comment: Belatedly came good last year with a string of placed efforts and a win over 9.5f here

runners.name: Hell Left Loose
runners.spotlight: Old boy won easily over C&D on heavy ground in the autumn and close third on soft here on second-last start; well held over 7f back here since.
runners.comment: Close third over C&D (soft) on second-last start; well held over 7f back here since

runners.name: Verhoyen
runners.spotlight: Grand veteran probably best at 6f/7f and quiet so far this term.
runners.comment: Grand veteran probably best at 6f/7f and quiet so far this term
runners.owner: Paul Cullen & Joseph E Keeling & M C Grassick

races.race_name: CopyBet Supporting UK Racing Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mahler Moon
runners.spotlight: In great form on good ground this spring, winning twice and running well in between; raised only a further 2lb for Wincanton performance (3m1f) last time; likely to remain competitive.
runners.comment: In great form on good ground this spring and may well remain competitive

runners.name: The Gypsy Davey
runners.spotlight: Achieved nothing on return from layoff last time but may well prove resurgent returned to Hereford with the outing under his belt, being 3-3 here (2m/2m5f); new trip is the question mark.
runners.comment: 3-3 at Hereford (2m/2m5f); new trip is the question mark

runners.name: Petty Cash
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-8 over fences; has had wind surgery twice, yet made a respiratory noise in two of his last three outings; this may be the time to catch him, with tongue-tie applied for the first time.
runners.comment: This may be the time to catch him, with tongue-tie applied for the first time

runners.name: Edgewell
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-13 over fences; 2-2 over C&D, namely good-ground wins this spring; below par at Fontwell in hat-trick attempt but has possibilities back here; admittedly yet to score above Class 5.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D, namely wins this spring; yet to score above Class 5

runners.name: Smurfette
runners.spotlight: Consistent mare who is 3-11 over fences; ran creditably (close second) last time when bidding for a Southwell double (3m/3m2f); up another 3lb but again looks a solid contender.
runners.comment: Consistent mare who ran creditably last time when bidding for Southwell double; solid

runners.name: Maximum Offers
runners.spotlight: Beat the only other finisher by 35l over C&D (good) last month on third chase attempt and second start in cheekpieces; open to further progress in this sphere and warrants respect up just 2lb.
runners.comment: Beat the only other finisher by a wide margin over C&D last time; up just 2lb; respected
runners.owner: L H & A Snowden & J E Snowden

races.race_name: Safer Gambling At CopyBet Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Backer Bilk
runners.spotlight: Has shown most of his Flat form on AW, winning once; never landed a blow over C&D switched to hurdles last time but this looks a weaker race.
runners.comment: AW Flat winner; never landed a blow over C&D last time but this is weaker

runners.name: Whatcombe
runners.spotlight: Hasn't progressed from a good 2yo effort and he holds weak claims on Flat/hurdles form for current yard; however, may be capable of a resurgent display, with this contest being far from strong.
runners.comment: May be capable of a resurgent display, with this contest being far from strong

runners.name: Noble Don
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in points but looks far from solid on rules form; admittedly faces a more realistic task than at Newbury last time.
runners.comment: Far from solid on rules form but latest task was difficult

runners.name: Delta Legend
runners.spotlight: Raced mainly on AW, winning twice in 2024 then eventually regressing; best watched on stable/hurdles debut after a layoff.
runners.comment: Flat form eventually regressed; best watched on stable/hurdles debut after a layoff

runners.name: The Craftymaster
runners.spotlight: Hurdles debutant who is a nine-time AW winner at 1m6f/2m, most recently in March; one of the better contenders in terms of achievements but an unconvincing turf record leaves him with something to prove.
runners.comment: Nine AW wins; turf record leaves him with something to prove on hurdles debut
runners.owner: R Miles, V Miles, T Stamp & Partner

runners.name: Machete Beach
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who scored twice on Flat in 2024 for previous yard; absent since compromising his chance by taking a good hold in Plumpton maiden hurdle (2m, good; finished third) a year ago, but respected in terms of ability in this weak race and may show progress if he settles better.
runners.comment: Two Flat wins; absent since finishing third in Plumpton maiden hurdle a year ago

runners.name: Alex The Great
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner at a modest level on Flat; beaten long way over C&D switched to hurdles time; opposed.
runners.comment: AW Flat winner; beaten long way over C&D switched to hurdles last time

runners.name: Haderach
runners.spotlight: Dismal claims on form; combination of physical tweak (wind surgery) and fitting of tongue-tie/hood needs to make a huge difference.
runners.comment: Combination of wind surgery and fitting of aids needs to make a huge difference

races.race_name: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: The Long Walk
runners.spotlight: Mixed form last season; peak efforts included third at Market Rasen (2m2f) on Boxing Day and most recently when runner-up over C&D last month, beaten 6.5l off this mark; not guaranteed to back that up but in the mix if doing so.
runners.comment: Mixed form this season but enters calculations based on his second over C&D last month.

runners.name: Double Click
runners.spotlight: Proved well suited by the step back up to 3m1f and good ground when keeping on well to score at Ffos Las last month; faces a different question here off 5lb higher but likely to be thereabouts if reproducing the effort.
runners.comment: 3m1f Ffos Las winner last time; now 5lb higher but still enters calculations.

runners.name: Queens Venture
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Dan Skelton with two wins last summer (about 2m6f); shaped well on stable debut when third at Newcastle in November but pulled up at Chepstow three weeks later, both at about 3m; off 150 days but she's won off similar breaks in the past and not ruled out if back on song.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Chepstow when last seen but bounce-back not ruled out returning from a break.

runners.name: Earth King
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles who had looked to be in the grip of the handicapper since wining at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) in July; however, he's now back on that winning mark and his latest fourth of four at Taunton (3m, good) in a Class 3 was encouraging; respected.
runners.comment: Back on latest winning mark and recent comeback in a higher class was encouraging.

runners.name: I Spy A Diva
runners.spotlight: Four wins over hurdles (at up to 3m) in 2022 and 2023; missed all of 2024; although yet to match that original level since returning from an absence in October, they have been largely respectable efforts and she's arguably weighted to win, if ready to go after a 124-day break.
runners.comment: Largely respectable efforts last season and arguably weighted to win if ready to go.

runners.name: Musique De Fee
runners.spotlight: Hasn't looked straightforward since finishing runner-up at Wetherby in October, pulling up on debut for this stable and well held back in this sphere at Perth last month; hard to recommend on current evidence.
runners.comment: Pulled up over fences and well held back in this sphere latest; hard to recommend.

runners.name: Chavez
runners.spotlight: Very well handicapped on some of his old form for Willie Mullins but, following a layoff, was soundly beaten on his two starts for Venetia Williams (both 3m); probably best watched at present.
runners.comment: Formerly useful but soundly beaten on both starts for this stable and best watched.

races.race_name: Wye Valley Metals Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Mount Anglesby
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of his six starts for this stable, most recently at Warwick (2m5f, good to soft) in March off 4lb lower, but didn't quite match that level when fourth (2m4f, good) at Plumpton last month; may need to find more but likely to be in the mix.
runners.comment: Failed to match the level of March's Warwick win latest, but likely to be in the mix.

runners.name: Oakley
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing sequence over C&D in November; below that level in three runs since, although recent return at Stratford was a little more encouraging; more needed off his current mark but he has place claims.
runners.comment: C&D winner in November but hasn't matched that level since and remains on a stiff mark.

runners.name: Cluain Chormaic
runners.spotlight: Progressive type who showed promise in two runs over hurdles in Ireland and has shaped well on both starts for this stable, making the frame on both occasions; latest was at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft) in April; enters handicaps with plenty of potential.
runners.comment: Has shaped with promise on all four runs over hurdles and brings potential to handicaps.

runners.name: Donnie Azoff
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner, including over 2m5f here in October when ridden by Isabelle Ryder, form which gives him a solid chance; beaten 35l at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft) in November and needs to bounce back but he's won off a similar break in the past.
runners.comment: Needs to bounce back from a poor in November but he's won off a similar break before.

runners.name: American Sniper
runners.spotlight: Has become hard to predict, as his 16l fourth at Taunton (2m3f, good) in January would suggest, having failed to back up a decent effort there the previous month; back on his last winning mark and could play a leading role if at his best.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but could play a leading role off a good mark if at his best.

runners.name: Pike Road
runners.spotlight: Thorough stayer, a dual winner over 2m7f last season, latterly on soft ground at Uttoxeter in November; dropped in class at Southwell on latest start but proved unsuited by the drop to 2m4f, and this trip is likely to find him out again.
runners.comment: Thorough stayer who was unsuited down in trip latest and that could find him out again.

runners.name: Individualiste
runners.spotlight: Lost his way over fences in Ireland and showed no signs of an immediate revival when pulled up reverting to hurdles on stable debut in March; too much to prove at present.
runners.comment: Lost his way in Ireland and offered little on March's stable debut; too much to prove.

races.race_name: Green Dragon Hotel Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: American Empire
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who made the frame in his first two runs over hurdles before a disappointing effort at Fakenham (2m, good to soft) in October, after which he underwent wind surgery; returned from a break to finish a solid third over C&D last month and, still unexposed, may have more to offer now switching to handicaps.
runners.comment: Returned from a break following a wind op with a solid third over C&D last month; player.

runners.name: Ettore
runners.spotlight: Third over hurdles at Taunton (extended 2m, good) in March but failed to match that level on starts either side; cheekpieces are now applied for handicap debut and, while his jumping has lacked fluency at times, he's hard to dismiss representing top stable.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces go on for handicap debut and he's hard to dismiss representing top stable.
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late John Connolly

runners.name: Triple Haych
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point but has achieved very little over hurdles (2m-2m3f), including on first completed start in handicaps when beaten 39l at Chepstow last month; difficult to make a case for.
runners.comment: Has achieved little over hurdles and it's difficult to make a case for him.

runners.name: Not So Sobers
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability in four runs over hurdles but was beaten a long way on handicap hurdle debut at Exeter (2m, good) in November; down 3lb but with something to prove on the back of that effort; modest form on Flat since, towards rear ten days ago on latest start.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on handicap hurdle debut in November; modest on Flat since.

runners.name: Tao Enki
runners.spotlight: Showed ability on three of his four runs over hurdles but looks on a stiff mark, despite a 4lb drop, judged on his recent handicap debut; now tries headgear.
runners.comment: Looks on stiff mark, despite 4lb drop, judged on his recent handicap debut; headgear now.

runners.name: Southey
runners.spotlight: Showed promise on five starts over hurdles, making the frame on three occasions, and took a clear step forward from chase debut when finishing a close second at Ayr (2m, soft) in January; has since left Stuart Crawford for 6,500gns and now returns to hurdles on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Returns to hurdles on a workable mark for new yard after an improved effort over fences.

races.race_name: Wye Valley Skips Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Kally Des Bruyeres
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who was having only his second run over fences when making all for a narrow success at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) one month ago ridden by Charlie Maggs; 5lb higher but that was a good performance and he remains open to improvement in this sphere; leading contender.
runners.comment: Produced a good performance from the front last time and the 5lb rise may not stop him.

runners.name: Away For The Night
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden over hurdles but already shaping as a better proposition over fences, building on an encouraging second on debut to get off the mark at Plumpton (2m1f, good) last month; 4lb rise looks fair given the likelihood of further progress and he can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Has taken well to fences and the 4lb rise for his recent win looks manageable.

runners.name: Daany
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner, all at around 2m; made a belated switch to fences last month and shaped with plenty of encouragement when runner-up at Wincanton (2m, good), which also represented a return to some form; needs to back that up but he remains well treated and is one to consider.
runners.comment: Back to some form when second on chase debut last month; well treated if backing it up.
