runners.owner: Duckhaven D Kenealy W Sprigg M Sprigg

runners.name: Prairie Queen
runners.spotlight: Still winless after 20 starts but placed over hurdles and fences at up to 2m4f (all on a sound surface); did too much too soon when failing to see it out at Exeter (2m, good) last time but had been running consistently well beforehand from this sort of mark and could have a part to play back over fences.
runners.comment: Best to forgive latest effort; on a fair mark and could figure reverting to fences.

runners.name: O'Grady's Hill
runners.spotlight: Best effort for current stable came when a well-held third at Taunton (2m2f, good) in March but her overall profile remains disappointing; the blinkers need to spark significant improvement.
runners.comment: Overall profile remains disappointing and connections are now reaching for blinkers.

runners.name: Danny's Friend
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last month when well supported on just his second chase start off 7lb lower, but failed to build on that next time when seemingly not seeing out 2m5f on good to soft ground; comes back in trip and a return to the level of his win would give him claims.
runners.comment: C&D winner who failed to see out 2m5f on good to soft last time; respected back in trip.

runners.name: Kill The Groove
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles though reaching the frame on three starts at Plumpton this year, all over 2m; made an underwhelming switch to fences last month at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft) trailing in last of the five finishers; needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles, though placed, but her recent chase debut was underwhelming.

runners.name: Fox Wallace
runners.spotlight: 2m1f chase winner at Stratford a year ago but has largely struggled to reproduce that level, including after wind surgery; continues to ease in the weights and cheekpieces are enlisted, but others arrive with much more persuasive credentials.
runners.comment: Continues to struggle for form and the cheekpieces need to turn things around.
runners.owner: M Enticott / Ella Pickard Racing Club

runners.name: Mi Sueno
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground hurdle winner (handles good) in early 2024 but had been badly out of sorts prior to his second at Taunton (2m, good) last month; now switches to fences and needs to build on that revival, though he remains attractively treated on old form.
runners.comment: Well treated but needs to build on a return to form last time; now switched to fences.

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Bossy Parker
runners.spotlight: Winning for the third time at Wolverhampton, and his first over further than 7f, when landing a C&D handicap in March; his stamina looked stretched by 9.4f when fourth 18 days ago; not ruled out back down in distance.
runners.comment: C&D winner in March and then stretched by 9.4f last month; one to consider

runners.name: Shielas Well
runners.spotlight: She should be well handicapped now and her last two runs have both been respectable; cheekpieces tried last time are now replaced by first-time blinkers; one to consider.
runners.comment: On a lowly mark and her last two runs have been okay; respected in new headgear

runners.name: Angel's Call
runners.spotlight: Course winner but over 5f; nothing to suggest he wants this far though and easy enough to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: His win came over 5f and his stamina is still a major query

runners.name: Al Baahy
runners.spotlight: Won twice in January, including over 9.4f here, and he is back to a winning mark after three disappointing runs this spring; not a solid option.
runners.comment: 2 wins over further in January; on winning mark but as a result of some modest recent runs

runners.name: Devious Devan
runners.spotlight: Thrown in on his 3yo form but he has gone the wrong way in a hurry and a wind operation last November hasn't arrested the slide; drops into his first 0-55 and has a good apprentice booked so a revival isn't completely out of the question.
runners.comment: Regressive 5yo; hopes are pinned on the drop to 0-55 company turning things around

runners.name: Street Dancer
runners.spotlight: Offered a bit more when a close fifth of eight over 7f here two weeks ago, her first handicap run; speed on bottom line of her pedigree but she is by Cracksman so this step up in trip could help.
runners.comment: Some promise on handicap debut here two weeks ago (7f); new trip could spark extra

runners.name: Prince Ali
runners.spotlight: Four course wins, including two over this trip, and he ran his best race for a while when third over C&D four weeks ago; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit and ran a sound race over C&D four weeks ago; each-way shout

runners.name: Fumbleinthejungle
runners.spotlight: Exposed 13-race maiden who has offered little for this yard after a mammoth absence.
runners.comment: Exposed 13-race maiden who has offered little for this yard after a mammoth absence

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Guesstimate
runners.spotlight: Promise in two runs last year (7f and 1m); raced too freely and dropped right away when the 2-1 favourite for a Kempton maiden (1m) on last month's return to action; not much to find to play a leading role but she will need to settle better over today's extra yardage.
runners.comment: Promise as a 2yo; too free on last month's reappearance; capable of better

runners.name: Bintaziza
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite for an ordinary Newcastle maiden (1m) two weeks ago and she ran out a wide-margin winner; she has more on her plate under a penalty but she still commands plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism at Newcastle in good style last month; big player despite penalty

runners.name: Romance Spirit
runners.spotlight: Promising second at Southwell on last month's debut (1m; form franked); disappointed as odds-on favourite at Ripon ten days ago (1m2f) when that tricky course didn't appear to suit; could fare better back at a more conventional track.
runners.comment: Debut was promising (1m, AW); failed to fire at Ripon ten days ago but could bounce back

runners.name: Wosta
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to winners Alsaba (French 1m1f; RPR 93) and Thyer (1m including 2yo; 89); dam 1m winner (89); lots to like on paper and one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two fair winners out of a 1m winner; a newcomer of some interest

runners.name: Midnight Media
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise on her second run (1m, Polytrack); failed to back it up returned to Kempton last month; allotted a BHA mark of 62 and she would make more appeal in a minor handicap.
runners.comment: Second run was promising; she would be of more interest in a minor handicap though

runners.name: Golden Mabel
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in two fillies' novices this spring; no appeal.
runners.comment: Huge prices and well beaten in two fillies' novices this spring; no appeal

runners.name: Symphony's Song
runners.spotlight: Modest form in a 7f maiden at Newcastle in one run for George Boughey last March; non-runner 2.58 Windsor Monday.
runners.comment: Modest form in one run for George Boughey 425 days ago; non-runner 2.58 Windsor Monday

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Lexington Express
runners.spotlight: 0-9 for Alice Haynes but she has won two of her five starts for Marco Botti, both front-running wins on AW; 3lb rise for narrow win 12 days ago asks a stiffer question but she should enjoy the run of the race.
runners.comment: On the up this spring, winning two of her last three; easy lead a possibility; contender

runners.name: Tinsel
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when readily seeing off Zipwire over C&D in December; that rival has improved since but these weights offer him a good chance of confirming the placings; non-runner 3.33 Windsor Monday.
runners.comment: Easily saw off Ziipwire over C&D in December and 3lb better off today; non-runner Monday
runners.owner: A Bevan, I McMillan & Glassex Holdings

runners.name: Zipwire
runners.spotlight: He had looked exposed prior to headgear going on in December but it sparked improvement, winning over 8.6f in December and following up over C&D in March; came up short in his hat-trick bid at Chelmsford (1m2f) 16 days ago but still ran well; respected.
runners.comment: Two wins, including C&D, and a second since cheekpieces went on; should go well again
runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore

runners.name: Mimi's Magic
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of her four starts, the latest when third to Bintaziza (runs in the 7.00) at Newcastle two weeks ago (1m); needs more now handicapping but it is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Placed twice from four starts; improvement needed now handicapping but it's not impossible

races.race_name: Download The Racecourse App Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Amber Hamur
runners.spotlight: Yet to progress from her debut effort and she weakened into a 6l fourth at Kempton on March's handicap debut (7f; sent off 7-2); can do better and it will be interesting to see if her backers return.
runners.comment: Weakened into 4th on handicap debut in March (7f, Polytrack); still has time to do better
runners.owner: G C Myddelton & V E C Taylor

runners.name: Amazing Anita
runners.spotlight: Ran a solid race at Southwell on her reappearance in March (1m; cheekpieces first time) but she hasn't built on it in two runs since; drawn wide and could prove vulnerable again.
runners.comment: Promising reappearance in March but no progress in two runs since; tricky draw; opposable

runners.name: Musical Soldier
runners.spotlight: Has failed to make the frame in his six outings to date, only seventh of nine in an 8.6f Wolverhampton handicap six weeks ago; drops back to 7f with improvement.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six starts; drops back to 7f with improvement required

runners.name: Castlekeely
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three 6f novice runs for Eve Johnson Houghton last year; sent off the 4-1 favourite for his stable/handicap debut at Leicester three weeks ago (7f, good) but never featured; no better back at Leicester ten days ago but that was in a Class 5; down in the weights and his best RPR came in his only AW start so it's too soon to discount him completely.
runners.comment: Best RPR came on his only AW run; risky but not impossible for his in-form stable

runners.name: Poetic Grace
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three runs in the second half of 2025; better form when fourth of nine in a 1m handicap at Yarmouth (blinkered first time) two weeks ago; capable of better.
runners.comment: Last month's handicap debut was encouraging; open to further progress

runners.name: Amakhala
runners.spotlight: Poor form in four handicap runs this year; hard to fancy back up to 7f.
runners.comment: Poor form in four handicap runs this year; hard to fancy back up to 7f

runners.name: Apex Star
runners.spotlight: Third of five, beaten 5.25l, behind the red-hot favourite over C&D last month; needs to raise his game a level to win but he does at least bring low mileage.
runners.comment: Third of five over C&D on handicap debut last month; low mileage but needs more to win

runners.name: Evolve
runners.spotlight: Promise in maiden/novice runs but he has been well beaten in two handicap runs (gelded after the first of them); down in grade and cheekpieces are added but he is not a solid option.
runners.comment: Yet to shine in handicaps; drops in grade with cheekpieces added; risky

runners.name: Power Of Chora
runners.spotlight: Never going the pace on her handicap debut nine days ago (6f, good) but she did plug on well, beaten 3.5l at the finish; stepping back up in trip should suit but she will need to pull out more to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Late progress over 6f on recent handicap debut; unexposed but improvement essential

runners.name: Bear Steps
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three 7f runs in novice/maiden company; there was money for her on debut and she could take a step forward in handicaps but the widest stall dents confidence today.
runners.comment: Potential improver now handicapping but stall 12 dents confidence today

runners.name: Pink Diamond Girl
runners.spotlight: Similar form in four starts, weakening into seventh on her handicap debut over C&D seven weeks ago; well drawn to attack but she will need to raise her game.
runners.comment: Weakened into 7th over C&D on March's handicap debut; low mileage but improvement required

runners.name: East India Breeze
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 4-1 second favourite for his handicap debut at Lingfield in January but he ended up last of the nine runners (7f); now tried in blinkers.
runners.comment: Flopped when one of the market leaders on handicap debut in January; now tried in blinkers

races.race_name: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Kento
runners.spotlight: In fine form on AW this year, winning twice over C&D and placed in his other four starts; record when ridden by Rossa Ryan is 333113; leading contender.
runners.comment: Two wins and four placed efforts from six runs this year; goes well for Rossa Ryan; solid

runners.name: Colors Of Freedom
runners.spotlight: Hard to knock her consistency since winning over C&D last June but her losing run is up to 22; should run her race again but more minor money could be here best hope.
runners.comment: Consistent mare who goes well over C&D; losing run has crept up to 22 though

runners.name: Isla Bella
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner but conditions suit and she ran well for third over C&D eight days ago; each-way shout once again.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but conditions suit and she ran well here eight days ago; each-way claims

runners.name: Black Cab
runners.spotlight: Placed in two 6f 0-50 classified events here in March; slowly away last time, however, and won't be able to get away with that back at 5f.
runners.comment: Placed in two classified events here (6f) in March; can't get the start wrong back at 5f

runners.name: Vault Of Heaven
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; has attracted support on a few occasions, including at Doncaster on her seasonal return, and could yet have a bigger run in him; blinkers replace the hood or cheekpieces worn on his last three starts.
runners.comment: 0-13 but often backed and could still have a bigger run in him; now tried in blinkers

runners.name: Trust Sergei
runners.spotlight: Disappointed as favourite in a 6f handicap here six weeks ago but he is well handicapped on his 3yo best and he was notably short in the betting prior to being withdrawn at Doncaster last month (bolted to post); headstrong sort but more than capable of playing a role if not overdoing it in the preliminaries.
runners.comment: Well-handicapped C&D winner; bolted to post and withdrawn at Doncaster last month; chance

runners.name: Tomarlo
runners.spotlight: Fair AW form when trained in Ireland, recording an RPR of 69 as recently as November; ran poorly in his three starts for Barry Brennan this year but he has dropped 9lb for his troubles and starts out for a new yard at a low level; tongue-tie added; interesting.
runners.comment: Thrown in on Irish AW form; starts out for new yard off a reduced mark; interesting

runners.name: Lismacbryan Hill
runners.spotlight: Her wins in Ireland came on the beach at Laytown (6f) and in a Dundalk handicap (6f); makes her stable debut after 139 days off; drawn widest but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Not on a bad mark now starting out for a new stable; both wins have come at 6f; drawn wide

races.race_name: Get The Inside Track With raceday-ready.com Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Magna
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who has won four of her seven starts since dropped back to sprint trips, including C&D; 6lb higher mark to contend with but she is 2-2 for Rossa Ryan and looks booked for another big run.
runners.comment: Won four of her last seven starts, including C&D; up in the weights but still improving

runners.name: Daytona Lady
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways when seeing off five rivals over C&D (her third C&D success) two weeks ago; a 2lb rise doesn't handicap her out of things but she will need a good pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit and she won well over C&D two weeks ago; strong early pace would help
runners.owner: Eric Broadwith And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: Moostar
runners.spotlight: Four runs in a tongue tie have seen her record formfigures of 2323; now has a visor added but she has been well beaten when tried in cheekpieces in the past.
runners.comment: Four good runs since a tongue tie was added; now tried in a visor; respected

runners.name: Court Drive
runners.spotlight: Has claims on this winter's best and she didn't get a clear shot at it over C&D when last seen nine weeks ago; others still appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Has claims on this winter's best but others look more persuasive for win purposes

runners.name: Skellig Isle
runners.spotlight: Came from well back to win a 5f handicap at Chelmsford on her stable debut in March; unable to reproduce that form at Yarmouth last month; still has handicapping scope and returning to 6f/Class 5 can help.
runners.comment: Not at best at Yarmouth last month but still has handicapping scope and this is easier

runners.name: So Sassy
runners.spotlight: Looked one to keep on the right side of when sweeping through late to win at Lingfield last summer (6f, AW); generally held her form since and she is now back to her winning mark; drops in grade today and she's a key player, albeit she could do with them going hard early.
runners.comment: On a winning mark and today's drop in grade will help; good chance if they go quick early

races.race_name: Prix Greffulhe (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Hello Jo
runners.spotlight: Progressing with racing on the AW and produced by far his best effort last time when winning a 1m1f Listed event on Polytrack at Chantilly, beating a rival who won a Group 3 next time; has the potential for better with the only issue to address is whether he can run to the same level on turf; doesn't hold any Group 1 entries.
runners.comment: Won an AW Listed event last time that has worked out well; needs to act on grass
runners.owner: Pegase Bloodstock, Fal Stud Sas Et Al

runners.name: Regal Resolve
runners.spotlight: Behind Waybreaker on debut last August but won next two, both on the AW, the last at Listed level, before finishing fifth in last month's Group 3 Prix de Fontainebleau (1m, good), shaping better than his position; sound claims if running to similar level.
runners.comment: Ran well despite being fifth last time; sound claims on the figures

runners.name: Waybreaker
runners.spotlight: Had Regal Resolve behind when winning on debut and went on to land a Group 3 here over a bit shorter last October on soft ground when in the care of Christopher Head; hasn't stepped up massively from that since on the figures and, while not dismissed given the field size, a bit more exposed than his rivals.
runners.comment: Has a good level of form but bit more exposed than his rivals

runners.name: Alam
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred colt who has shown plenty of promise but hasn't yet won a race; only beaten a head on his turf debut 32 days ago over C&D on soft but the figures leave him with a little bit to find.
runners.comment: Only one of these that hasn't won but retains potential for better

races.race_name: Prix Greffulhe (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Hello Jo
runners.spotlight: Progressing with racing on the AW and produced by far his best effort last time when winning a 1m1f Listed event on Polytrack at Chantilly, beating a rival who won a Group 3 next time; has the potential for better with the only issue to address is whether he can run to the same level on turf; doesn't hold any Group 1 entries.
runners.comment: Won an AW Listed event last time that has worked out well; needs to act on grass
runners.owner: Pegase Bloodstock, Fal Stud Sas Et Al

runners.name: Regal Resolve
runners.spotlight: Behind Waybreaker on debut last August but won next two, both on the AW, the last at Listed level, before finishing fifth in last month's Group 3 Prix de Fontainebleau (1m, good), shaping better than his position; sound claims if running to similar level.
runners.comment: Ran well despite being fifth last time; sound claims on the figures

runners.name: Waybreaker
runners.spotlight: Had Regal Resolve behind when winning on debut and went on to land a Group 3 here over a bit shorter last October on soft ground when in the care of Christopher Head; hasn't stepped up massively from that since on the figures and, while not dismissed given the field size, a bit more exposed than his rivals.
runners.comment: Has a good level of form but bit more exposed than his rivals

runners.name: Alam
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred colt who has shown plenty of promise but hasn't yet won a race; only beaten a head on his turf debut 32 days ago over C&D on soft but the figures leave him with a little bit to find.
runners.comment: Only one of these that hasn't won but retains potential for better

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Vintage Tipple Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Snapretend
runners.spotlight: Won twice last season, a Punchestown maiden (9.5f, gd-yld) and a conditions race at Bellewstown (12.5f, soft); reeled in late on by Moody in Cork Listed race on return (1m4f, sft-hvy); 3lb better off for that 0.5l defeat so could turn that form around but not guaranteed to stay 1m6f on breeding; the choice of McMonagle.
runners.comment: 3lb better off for 0.5l defeat by Moody latest, stamina to prove, preferred by McMonagle

runners.name: Floresta
runners.spotlight: Progressive last season; two wins from five starts including a Listed contest at Leopardstown (1m6f, good) by 4.25l; signed off with a very respectable 3.75l fifth in the Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster (14.5f, gd-sft); carries a penalty but big chance if ready after 236 days off.
runners.comment: Progressive, Listed win last year, fine effort in Gr'2 Park Hill, 3lb penalty, big chance

runners.name: Moody
runners.spotlight: French recruit got off to a perfect start for Paddy Twomey, landing a Listed heat at Cork (1m4f, sft-hvy), beating Snapretend by 0.5l; 3lb worse off with that rival now and is moving up in trip but her dam won over 1m6f so there's every chance she'll stay; headgear worn last time is retained.
runners.comment: Won 1m4f Cork Listed on stable debut, should stay this far, carries 3lb penalty, chance

runners.name: Nuit
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been highly tried at times to no avail but did pick up some black type when a 5.25l third behind Floresta in a Listed heat at Leopardstown (1m6f, good) last season; beaten in a Dundalk maiden when last seen in December; ground a concern on return; tongue tie goes on.
runners.comment: Maiden, Listed placed behind Floresta last season, ground a query here, tongue tied

runners.name: Jannah Star
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and rated 76; placed in all four maidens last year at 1m4f on varying ground; fourth in Bellewstown maiden latest; steps up in trip and class now; hard to make a case for on form.
runners.comment: 0-6 and rated 76, beaten in Bellewstown maiden latest, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Bay Colony
runners.spotlight: 1-8 on the turf, the win coming in a 1m4f Limerick maiden last season (1m4f, sft-hvy); decent run in premier handicap at the Curragh last time (1m2f, sft-hvy) but much more needed in this company; well beaten four previous starts in stakes races and stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Maiden winner, decent handicap run on latest, much more needed, stamina to prove

runners.name: Dippedinmoonlight
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; point-to-point and bumper winner; 1-3 on the Flat; beaten off 86 in a 1m6f handicap at Navan (heavy) latest; major improvement needed in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, beaten off 86 in handicap on latest, lots more needed in new cheekpieces

runners.name: Contorsion
runners.spotlight: French recruit one of four for yard; won maiden by 10l on debut for Andre Fabre at Saint-Cloud (1m4f, soft); suffered odds on defeat stepped into Listed company at Longchamp (1m4f, gd-sft); bought for E100,000 at Arqana in December; new trip here and overlooked by McMonagle so likely best watched after 320-day absence.
runners.comment: French maiden winner, bought for E100,000, new trip, overlooked by McMonagle, watch

runners.name: Denim And Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Sole success in Limerick maiden last June (1m4f, good); didn't really progress from that and finished well beaten both tries in Listed company last year; below best on seasonal return in a handicap here (1m4f, soft); this looks too demanding.
runners.comment: Limerick maiden winner, struggled both previous starts in Listed company, up against it

runners.name: Tamam Desert
runners.spotlight: No win since landing a Galway maiden in 2024; well beaten on first two starts for this yard in Curragh handicaps at 1m and 1m2f on testing ground; hood worn for those runs discarded and upped in trip; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No win since 2024, well beaten in Curragh handicaps last twice, hard to fancy

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Victor McCalmont Memorial Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Faiyum
runners.spotlight: Won three of four starts last season, including a C&D Group 3 by 7l on heavy ground; also placed in a Naas Group 3 on good ground; solid return when a 2.5l third in the 1m Park Express at the Curragh last month; step back up to this trip a plus and should be too good for these, even giving weight away.
runners.comment: C&D Group 3 winner last year, pleasing run on return last month, the one to beat

runners.name: Lemsairbat
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate of 1-18 but has run plenty of solid races in defeat, including in Listed company; fine effort on seasonal return when 3l behind Beset in Listed heat at Naas (10.5f, soft); not beaten far when fifth of 12 in Longchamp Listed race latest (10.5f, gd-sft); place chance but overlooked by McMonagle.
runners.comment: 1-18 but lots of solid efforts in defeat including in Listed company, place chance
runners.owner: H R D McCalmont & Hamish McCalmont & Andrew N C Bengough

runners.name: Abbey Actress
runners.spotlight: Solid form in handicaps for John O'Donoghue; solid efforts in handicaps at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy) and Leopardstown (1m2f, yld-sft) on first two runs for this stable; up against it on the figures here but if there's heavy rain she could sneak into the money.
runners.comment: Solid form in handicaps, up against it on the figures here, best on very testing ground

runners.name: She's A Nation
runners.spotlight: Impressive 5l Curragh maiden winner over 1m (good) on debut last June; 10l third in heavy-ground C&D Group 3 next time out; trailed in last of 10 on seasonal return in Group 3 at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy) but was reportedly showing signs of being in season; tongue tie now goes on; could improve for return to a sounder surface.
runners.comment: 10l third in C&D Gr' 3 last year, poor seasonal return, return to better ground could help

runners.name: Apercu
runners.spotlight: Four turf wins have come in handicaps, the latest in May last year at the Curragh off 92 (1m2f, good); beaten 3l off 92 on return at the Curragh 11 days ago; has come up short when tried in stakes company, including when down the field in this race last season; needs more.
runners.comment: Four-time handicap winner, soundly beaten in this last year, needs more

runners.name: Lady Lilac
runners.spotlight: Killarney maiden winner touched off on seasonal return in handicap at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy) off 80; beaten 3l off 4lb higher at Limerick in first-time cheekpieces (discarded) 10 days ago; this looks too ambitious.
runners.comment: Maiden winner, rated 84, this looks too ambitious, cheekpieces worn latest discarded

runners.name: Sindria
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown maiden winner for Dermot Weld; no joy first two starts for this stable but off the mark on seasonal return in a 1m handicap here (soft) off 82; moves up in trip and steps into pattern company for the first time; interesting that McMonagle prefers her to Lemsairbat; place claims.
runners.comment: Impressive 1m winner here latest, up in trip and grade, preferred by McMonagle

runners.name: Um Wadi
runners.spotlight: AW winner is 0-8 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; rated just 77 and beaten in a Limerick handicap on latest; surely out of her depth in this company.
runners.comment: 0-8 on turf, beaten in Limerick handicap latest, rated 77, out of her depth here
runners.owner: Mark Feighery & Kevin Blake & Paul McKeon

runners.name: Subsonic
runners.spotlight: Got to within 0.5l of the top class Minnie Hauk on debut at Leopardstown in 2024 (1m, soft); off the mark on her fourth and final start of 2025 in a 1m2f Curragh maiden (yld-sft); rated 88 and this looks too big an ask on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Won Curragh maiden on final start last year, rated 88, this looks too big an ask
